steps.title: Recognize that the surface is at zero depth
steps.content: The submersible reaches the ocean surface when its depth equals 0 meters. We need to solve $g(x) = 0$ to find when this occurs.

steps.title: Read zeros directly from the factored form
steps.content: The function is already given in factored form: $$g(x) = -\frac{1}{52}(x+17)(x-31)$$ The constant factor $-\frac{1}{52}$ is nonzero, so $g(x) = 0$ requires $(x+17)(x-31) = 0$. The zeros are immediate: $x = -17$ or $x = 31$.

steps.title: Apply the domain constraint
steps.content: Since $x \gt 0$ (minutes after collecting the sample), the solution $x = -17$ is impossible in this context. The only valid time is $x = 31$ minutes, when the device reaches the surface. The answer is 31.
